name: Terrain Memory
why_non_testable: No sensor measures 'memory' — sensors measure current physical state. Memory is not a measurable physical quantity at any scale relevant to exploration.
scientific_residue: Current rock properties (conductivity, density, magnetism) DO reflect geological history. This is standard geology, not memory. The testable version — rocks retain physical signatures of formation processes — is already in use.
what_would_make_testable: Nothing as stated. Already testable once rephrased as measurable physical properties (conductivity, density, remanent magnetism), which is standard geophysics.

name: Conscious Subsurface Response
why_non_testable: No protocol can measure geological consciousness. There is no operationalizable definition of geological intent that produces testable predictions distinguishable from standard geophysical response.
scientific_residue: Subsurface properties DO modulate surface observables — but this is physics, not communication. Every measurable surface anomaly over a deposit is explainable by physical properties (density contrast, conductivity, reflectance) without invoking intent.
what_would_make_testable: Define a specific, measurable response to a specific stimulus. At that point it is indistinguishable from geophysics and no longer requires the consciousness framing.

name: Dowsing / Radiesthesia (Literal Mechanism)
intuitive_concept: Rods or pendulums detect subsurface features through a claimed non-physical mechanism
why_non_testable: No controlled double-blind study has demonstrated above-chance performance for the claimed mechanism. Existing studies show performance consistent with prior geological knowledge and ideomotor effect, not a novel detection mechanism.
scientific_residue: Experienced dowsers are often excellent field geologists who unconsciously read terrain, vegetation, drainage patterns, and outcrop geometry. This pattern recognition is valuable geological knowledge — but it is not the mechanism claimed. The geological knowledge itself is testable and valuable.
what_would_make_testable: A rigorous double-blind study with adequate statistical power demonstrating above-chance detection in conditions where geological cues are fully controlled. No such study exists.

name: Earth Frequency Mineral Detection
intuitive_concept: Specific passive Earth frequencies (e.g., Schumann resonances) indicate mineral presence at local scale
why_non_testable: Schumann resonances (~7.83 Hz fundamental) are global cavity resonances. Local modulation by mineral deposits at exploration scale has never been demonstrated in peer-reviewed literature. No accessible sensor captures local EM spectral anomalies at the required spatial resolution.
scientific_residue: EM methods (magnetotellurics, CSEM, TDEM) DO use frequency-dependent EM response to map subsurface conductivity. These are active or semi-active controlled-source methods, not passive Earth-frequency listening. They work and are used in real exploration — but they are not what the intuitive concept describes.
what_would_make_testable: A peer-reviewed study demonstrating statistically significant correlation between local passive EM spectral anomalies and mineral deposits at exploration scale, with replication at multiple sites.

name: Biological Sensitivity to Ore Bodies
intuitive_concept: Plants or animals 'sense' mineral deposits through a non-chemical mechanism at depth
why_non_testable: While geobotany is real and well-documented (certain plants preferentially colonise specific soil chemistries), the claim that organisms sense ore at depth through a non-chemical mechanism has no identified physical pathway and no controlled experimental support.
scientific_residue: Geobotanical indicators ARE used in exploration. Certain plant species are copper-tolerant and are found preferentially over Cu-rich soils. This is soil chemistry response detectable via NDVI anomalies and hyperspectral reflectance — both of which ARE in the spectral pipeline. The chemical pathway is testable and already partially validated.
what_would_make_testable: Already testable as geobotany: NDVI anomalies, chlorophyll fluorescence suppression, or hyperspectral alteration signatures over mineralized ground. These are in the frontier registry as separate hypotheses. The non-chemical depth-sensing claim specifically would require demonstration of above-chance biological response in double-blind conditions with chemical cues excluded.
status: NON_TESTABLE_AT_THIS_MOMENT as non-chemical depth-sensing claim. ALREADY_TESTABLE as geobotany via spectral pipeline.
